note: D6 recall reference: label-grounded coverage rules against the ethereum-vuln-dataset controlled vocabulary (docs/label_design.md). Replaces the prose coverage judgments of findings_map.json (deprecated). Two reviewable layers: (1) `domain` defines the in-domain denominator STRUCTURALLY (label + root_cause + attack_path + severity), no per-finding prose; (2) `rules` decide coverage per (label, root_cause) cell, each with the base property ids that would catch the class and a rationale. The harness (src/speca_lean4/recall.py) verifies every covered_by id is actually emitted in the real 01e with the same label; a rule pointing at a non-emitted property counts as NOT covered. Unlisted (label, root_cause) cells count as NOT covered (no_rule), so coverage growth is always a reviewed data change.

domain.note: D1: narrow start, grown gradually. v1 domain = the three consensus-specs areas the 25-theorem FFG target set maps to, restricted to root causes the FFG state-semantics model can express in principle (state-arithmetic / state-semantics divergence), attacker-triggerable paths, and rows the dataset rates as actual findings (severity != Info; the Info rows in this slice are changelog/dependency-bump crawl lines, not protocol findings). Growth path: v2 adds missing_input_validation / unhandled_error_or_nil causes and adjacent labels (beacon-chain:attestation) once matching invariants exist.

exclusion_reasons.root_cause: resource_exhaustion (OOM/DoS), race_condition (concurrency implementation), crypto_misuse (crypto library) and other/unclassified are outside the FFG formal remit per impl plan section 4 — same classes the deprecated findings_map excluded by prose.
exclusion_reasons.attack_path: peer (p2p transport), large_input (resource class) and internal_only are not consensus-state-transition triggers.
exclusion_reasons.severity: Info rows in the dataset are informational crawl entries (release-note lines, dependency bumps), not vulnerability findings; counting them would corrupt the denominator in both directions.

rules.label: beacon-chain:justification-and-finality
rules.rationale: Divergent justification/finalization bookkeeping violates the finalized/justified characterizations (finalized_means_justified_child, k_finalized_means_justified, finalized_means_one_finalized) and the k_safety' guarantee; a differential checker recomputing the canonical predicates flags the divergence.

rules.label: beacon-chain:justification-and-finality
rules.rationale: Overflow in justification-bit / checkpoint arithmetic makes the implementation's justified/finalized predicate diverge from the bounded-chain characterization (justified_iff_bounded) and the justification bookkeeping invariants; recomputing the canonical predicate over the same state flags it (e.g. nimbus PR#461, an overflow in exactly the justification-bits processing these theorems characterize).

rules.label: beacon-chain:justification-and-finality
rules.rationale: justified_iff_bounded proves justification is decidable by a BOUNDED chain search; unbounded iteration in the justification/finality computation violates the bounded characterization (same grounds as the prior lighthouse-unbounded-slot-counter partial judgment).

rules.label: beacon-chain:effective-balance-updates
rules.rationale: The Core SlashableBound trio pins exact validator-set weight arithmetic wt(V) and its churn-adjusted lower bounds; an effective-balance computation that diverges from the network shifts wt() and is flagged by a differential weight check (same grounds as the prior GHSA-wm9c-xvqq-5c28 full-coverage judgment).

rules.label: beacon-chain:effective-balance-updates
rules.rationale: Overflow/underflow in effective-balance arithmetic is a special case of wt() divergence pinned by the SlashableBound weight arithmetic.

rules.label: beacon-chain:slashing
rules.rationale: The S1/S2 decidable checkers are Iff characterizations of exactly which vote pairs are slashable (double vote / surround vote); an implementation whose equivocation/slashing semantics diverge (which messages count as slashable) contradicts the characterization under a differential check.

rules.label: beacon-chain:slashing
rules.rationale: gasper models weights and counters as unbounded naturals and ASSUMES exact arithmetic; fixed-width representation overflow (uint64-as-JS-number, CVE-2022-29219) has no counterpart invariant — the theorems assume exactness rather than establish it. Consistent with the deprecated prose judgment (lodestar-slashing-uint64-as-js-number: coverage none). Growth targets recorded in recall_gaps.json (new_target).

rules.label: beacon-chain:slashing
rules.rationale: The in-domain rows in this cell are bounds checks on API/request surfaces and slashing-protection-DB lookups, not consensus state-transition semantics; no mapped theorem constrains them. Per-row triage in recall_gaps.json.
